events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: a click wrench used upside down/uncalibrated during in-progress torque & threaded fastening work. This is non-compliant. Using a click wrench upside down can affect torque accuracy, and using an uncalibrated torque wrench is a quality gate violation. Decision should be fail. Risk is high because fasteners may be under/over-torqued, leading to joint failure. Component conditions: non-compliant, possibly installed-defective if fasteners were installed with wrong torque. Work stage: in-progress. Percent complete: maybe 50. Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked). Confidence high. Findings: click wrench used upside down and uncalibrated, torque values unreliable. Actions: stop work, remove from service, recalibrate/replace wrench, verify/rework affected fasteners to OEM specs. Rationale: torque wrench calibration and orientation matter; uncalibrated tool invalidates torque quality gate. References: OEM/engineering specs.
